title: Onboarding clobbered bee's own .codex/hooks.json on every self-onboard — two renderers, one generated file
action: onboard_bee.mjs does NOT import hooks/catalog.mjs — it carries a hand-copied duplicate (renderCodexHookEntries) that has drifted. catalog.mjs:76 renders `"$r"/hooks/<script>` for the codex repo target (correct for bee, whose hooks live in hooks/); onboard renders `"$r"/.bee/bin/hooks/<script>` (correct for a HOST, which has no hooks/ dir, only the vendored copy). Self-onboarding beegog wrote the HOST projection over bee's own catalog rendering, turning test_hook_contracts.mjs red with 22 failures. It had already happened once today and was misdiagnosed as a hand-edit; the real culprit is onboarding, which re-breaks it on every run — including the run that the release flow REQUIRES. Fix: repoOwnsHookCatalog() — a repo that ships hooks/catalog.mjs owns its own .codex/hooks.json, and onboarding never plans or writes it there. Same self-skip principle the skill sync already applies, keyed on the file that makes a repo the catalog's owner rather than on where the script runs from (so it holds even when onboarding runs from the global skill root). Host repos still get the projection — proven by a falsifiability row.

truths: A repo owning hooks/catalog.mjs never has .codex/hooks.json planned or written by onboarding
The catalog's rendering survives self-onboard byte-for-byte, and no .bak is written
A HOST repo still receives the .bee/bin/hooks/ projection — the skip is scoped, not a kill
The regression rows are falsifiable: breaking the fix turns 4 of them red
prohibitions: Do NOT hand-repair .codex/hooks.json again — that is what failed last time

verify: node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs >/dev/null && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs >/dev/null && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d['status']=='up_to_date', d['status']; assert not d.get('plan'), d['plan']; print('NO_CLOBBER_OK')"

trace.verification_evidence:
NO_CLOBBER_OK
verify exit=0

Root cause (measured, not inferred): onboard_bee.mjs does NOT import hooks/catalog.mjs.
  hooks/catalog.mjs:76          -> exec node "$r"/hooks/<script>          (bee's own repo: hooks live in hooks/)
  onboard_bee.mjs:1349          -> exec node "$r"/.bee/bin/hooks/<script> (HOST repo: only the vendored copy exists)
Self-onboarding beegog wrote the HOST projection over bee's own catalog rendering ->
hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs: "141 rows ... 22 failing target contract / 22 FAILURE(S)".

This had already happened once today and was misdiagnosed as a hand-edit. It was not.
Proof it is onboarding: `git checkout .codex/hooks.json` (restore) -> suite ALL PASS;
then `onboard --json` -> status=changes_needed, plan=[merge_codex_hooks]. A permanent loop:
verify is green ONLY with the catalog rendering, and onboarding ALWAYS wants to replace it.
The release flow requires onboarding, so the next release re-broke it -- which is exactly
what happened while cutting 0.1.42.

Fix: repoOwnsHookCatalog() -- a repo shipping hooks/catalog.mjs owns its .codex/hooks.json.
Keyed on the FILE (not on where the script runs from), so it holds when onboarding is
launched from the global skill root too.

AFTER:
  onboard --json -> status: up_to_date, plan: []
  .codex/hooks.json -> untouched (git diff empty)
  hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs -> 141 rows, 0 failing, ALL PASS
  FULL VERIFY chain -> exit 0

Falsifiability PROVEN (twice): setting repoOwnsHookCatalog() -> false reproduces the
pre-fix behavior and turns 4 of the 6 new rows RED; restoring returns them to green.
One row ("never PLANS merge_codex_hooks") was initially VACUOUS -- it stayed green with the
fix broken because it asserted against an --apply payload. Rewritten to assert the DRY-RUN
plan; it now goes red with the others (3 red -> 4 red).

New rows (skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs):
  - a repo owning hooks/catalog.mjs never PLANS merge_codex_hooks
  - the catalog's rendering survives self-onboard BYTE-FOR-BYTE (no clobber, no .bak)
  - no .bak is written for a file onboarding must not touch
  - the HOST projection (.bee/bin/hooks/) never leaks into bee's own repo
  - a HOST repo STILL receives the .bee/bin/hooks/ projection (the skip is scoped, not a kill)
